spanGrp.span: First of all there is something called ḥida
What is the ḥida?
It is as a commission of people coming to a wedding
And as I told you, when you invite me to a wedding, I don't bring you a symbolic present.
(But rather) I bring a present with an actual value.
I bring a ram.
The ram is like a big sheep
I bring a proper animal for slaughter.
and this "ḥaḏi" sometimes is "ġazal" (lit.eulogy in verses).
other times is "taḥaddi" (lit.challenge).
and others just normal poetry.
"ġazal", for instance, he would say to her...
With the tune I am going to sing it...
"Walk with me, walk with me
Dont you leave #######
Come with me to the plains
Oh, you that wear kohl in the eyes"
Between line and line, I stop.
I say "Walk with me, walk with me...", I stop, and the one behind me repeats the same sentence.
I continue and finish my lines, but they go back and repeat the line I said at the beginning.
This is "ġazal".
"taḥaddi", for instance, happens when there is something between the families...
there is some ####### between the families
so they say: "Oh soldier, gather your soldiers together
When you ####### towards our group"
There is also the "ġazal" with arrogance, like showing off.
He says to her:
"Oh child of mine behind the roofs
"Oh child of mine behind the roofs
Pass by and see our horses
up it is la turhuǧi
raising a
raising a
